steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content:
Sentence 1: rings outside the Roche limit are expected to accrete into a satellite.
Sentence 2: Morgado's team detected a ring well outside the Roche limit that has remained intact — contrary to expectation.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is contrast. Sentence 1 states the prediction; Sentence 2 presents a finding that defies it. The blank needs a transition signaling that the observation contradicts the rule.
  • Choice A — "likewise,": signals similarity — but the finding contradicts the prediction rather than confirming it. ✗
  • Choice B — "for example,": signals that Sentence 2 is a specific instance illustrating Sentence 1 — but a ring that stayed intact is the opposite of what Sentence 1 predicts. ✗
  • Choice C — "fittingly,": signals that the outcome is in line with expectations — but the ring's persistence is precisely what was not expected. ✗
  • Choice D — "though,": signals contrast — the ring defied the prediction by remaining intact rather than accreting. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
